steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The sentence immediately tells us how the three perspectives work: “The first section of the novel is from the mother Mina's perspective, the second is from the father Jun's perspective, and the last is from the daughter Nora's perspective.” This shows three separate, different viewpoints.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Distinct means clearly different or separate, which captures how each family member provides a unique perspective.

The other three—required (necessity), unintended (planning), unknown (knowledge)—refer to different concepts altogether.

That matches Choice A — distinct.
